items.title: ML leadership with production responsibility
items.summary: I work at the point where machine learning stops being a promising idea and becomes something a business can rely on. That includes team leadership, delivery standards, and making sure the work survives contact with reality.
bullets: Leading end-to-end ML work tied to meaningful business scope
Balancing model quality, operational constraints, and stakeholder needs
Building teams and practices that keep good work repeatable

items.title: AI applications beyond the demo layer
items.summary: I’m interested in AI systems that are genuinely useful, not just impressive in a screenshot. That means evaluation, iteration, and designing workflows that can hold up outside of a controlled demo.
bullets: Agent workflows and applied LLM systems
A bias toward utility, reliability, and grounded product value

items.title: Systems, cloud, and MLOps thinking
items.summary: I care a lot about the systems around the models: orchestration, reproducibility, cloud platforms, deployment patterns, and the operational layer that turns technical work into something durable.
bullets: Hands-on work across AWS, Azure, and GCP
Experience with SageMaker, ETL pipelines, analytics platforms, and production workflows
Strong focus on clean operational foundations, not just model output

items.title: Teaching and explanation as leverage
items.summary: Teaching is not a side note in my profile. It reflects how I work in general: make difficult things understandable, help people build confidence, and create momentum through explanation.
bullets: University teaching in statistics and introductory data science
Comfort translating technical depth for different audiences
A natural tendency to mentor, structure, and clarify

items.title: Leadership with business context
items.summary: My background in economics and management shapes how I lead. I tend to think in incentives, tradeoffs, uncertainty, and organizational constraints, which helps connect technical choices to business reality.
bullets: Economics roots still visible in how I frame problems
Entrepreneurial and management experience beyond pure technical execution
Comfort moving between strategic questions and implementation detail

items.title: Breadth that still feels coherent
items.summary: The common thread across my work is pretty simple: I like building systems, helping people grow, and making technical work useful. The domains vary, but the operating style is consistent.
bullets: Technical depth without losing the human layer
Strong pull toward structure, rigor, and usefulness
A profile shaped by execution, teaching, and systems thinking together
